calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reasonable Attorneys' Fees And Costs Pursuant To Labor Code Sec 98.2(C)
Off Calendar Feb-10-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. EMMANUEL ENYINWA, Tel No. (415) 956-6100 appearing for Appellant/Defendant Yiu Lon Wong dba Wong Yiu Construction Co.;
Atty. FERNANDO FLORES of the State of California, Department of Industrial Relations, Tel No. (415) 703-4814 appearing for Respondent/Plaintiff Hon Wei Chan.

RULING - A R GU E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. RESPONDENT HON WEI CHAN'S Motion For Reasonable Attorneys' Fees And Costs Pursuant To Labor Code Sec 98.2(C) is granted. Defendant Yiu Lun Wong shall pay Plaintiff Hon Wei Chan reasonable attorney's fees in the amount of $24,913.50 and costs in the amount of $1,602.00 within 30 days of entry of this order, pursuant to Lab. Code sec. 98.2(c). The plain language of Lab. Code sec. 98.2(c) provides that an employee is the prevailing party if the court amounts an award greater than zero. Plaintiff recovered $6,074.53 upon Defendant dismissing the appeal, and therefore, Plaintiff is entitled to recover fees and costs. Equitable principles support this result. Because Defendant filed a timely notice of appeal, Plaintiff was obligated to expend attorney's fees and costs through the date of trial. It would be inequitable to require Plaintiff to bear the burden of these costs. Further, Arias v. Kardoulias (2012) 207 Cal.App.4th 1429, the only case cited by Defendant in opposition, is inapposite. There, the trial court never obtained jurisdiction over the matter because the appellant did not timely file a notice of appeal. (Id. at 1438.) The court in Arias expressly limited its holding, stating that it "express[es] no opinion on the case in which a timely appeal from a commissioner's decision is dismissed on other grounds." (Id. at 1439, fn. 5.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reasonable Attorneys' Fees And Costs Pursuant To Labor Code Sec 98.2(C)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reasonable Attorneys' Fees And Costs Pursuant To Labor Code Sec 98.2(C)

MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. RESPONDENT HON CHAN'S Motion For Reasonable Attorneys' Fees and Costs Pursuant To Labor Code Sec 98.2(C) is continued to February 3, 2015, to give the opposing party a chance to comply with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. The opposing party shall immediately provide a courtesy copy of all opposing papers with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. The court has the moving papers. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/EHG)
